name: Source Fidelity review for Stem Cell Rejuvenation bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, and outlook claims match the checked primary records. ClinicalTrials.gov still lists NCT06877377 as a 60-participant not-yet-recruiting randomized hDNAgr trial with estimated 2026 completion dates and no posted results, while PubMed records PMID 42216078 as a 2026 Genome Biology HSC aging and rejuvenation-strategy review. The bundle does not treat either source as human efficacy evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged no-results finding matches the current registry boundary: NCT06877377 is not yet recruiting and has no results posted.
findings.why_it_matters: This keeps hDNAgr in the source-completeness and watchlist lane rather than implying demonstrated benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; continue ordinary surveillance for recruitment, status changes, results, or linked publications.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 42216078 supports HSC aging mechanism and rejuvenation-strategy context, not a treated-human result.
findings.why_it_matters: Review-level context can repair mechanism coverage but should not move the track beyond human biomarker signal.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; preserve the review-level caveat in public support mapping.
